regime: Holiday Drift / Uptrend Intact (Thin Juneteenth Tape)
summary: A quiet Juneteenth half-session that decided nothing and changed nothing. With US cash markets closed and CME equity futures shut at 1:00 PM ET, NQ and ES drifted in thin tape and held their post-FOMC repair: MNQ closed 30,651 (-0.22%) above its rising 30,461 ATR stop, MES 7,557 (-0.23%) above 7,526. TSMOM-20 stays armed long. Gold's 1hr stop flipped long on a marginal intraday reclaim, but it is net -1.30% on the day, a fragile thin-tape flip rather than a confirmed turn. Crude firmed +1.30% to 76.52. ATR(14) contracted again (MNQ 118.77 to 87.7); the only live risk now is the three-day-weekend gap into Monday June 22. Trade nothing you would not trade with a real read.

setups.name: Trend Continuation Long (Monday)
setups.entry: Monday only: long on a hold above the 30,461 rising stop; add on a dip toward 30,500 psych that holds on a 1hr basis
setups.t1: 30,705 RTH high, then 30,771 overnight high, then 31,000
setups.bias: Trend held through the holiday and TSMOM-20 stays armed long, but this is a post-holiday open with three-day gap risk. Demand a confirmed hourly hold; do not chase the first gap.

setups.name: Gold fragile-flip watch
setups.entry: Monday: only a hold above the 4,146 flipped stop on hourly closes keeps the thin-tape long honest
setups.bias: The 1hr flip is marginal and on holiday liquidity, and gold is still net down on the week. Treat it as a watch, not a signal, until Monday's real participation confirms or rejects it.

event.name: No scheduled release (Juneteenth federal holiday)
event.consensus: Holiday half-session; US cash closed, CME equity futures early close 1:00 PM ET. The only question was whether thin tape would give back the Jun 18 repair.
event.actual: It did not. NQ and ES drifted and held their stops; the post-FOMC repair survived on no participation.
event.surprise: None. A do-nothing holiday; the cleanest information was the absence of any reversal.
event.nqReaction: MNQ -0.22% to 30,651, holding above the rising 30,461 ATR stop all session. ATR(14) contracted to 87.7. TSMOM-20 stays armed long.
event.crossAsset: ES -0.23% (held its 7,526 stop). Gold -1.30% to 4,173, though its 1hr stop flipped long on a marginal reclaim (fragile, not confirmed). Crude +1.30% to 76.52, long firming above 74.74.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: Holiday tape is the wrong environment for a gap fade; the overnight was a two-way grind, not a gap-and-go. Monday's post-holiday open is the setup to watch.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: The 252-period filter stays aligned long with the short leg through the holiday. Watch for confirmation or divergence on Monday's reopen.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED (holiday). Bias toward NORMAL as event risk and volatility fade, but capped through the long weekend.
risk.ddProximity: NORMAL band; TSMOM-20 took no drawdown on the holiday.
risk.sizing: NQ and ES hold trend-following long habitat above their ATR stops; TSMOM-20 armed long. No new size on a holiday tape. Gold's flip is fragile, not a confirmed long. Crude long firming but not a holiday entry.
risk.note: Volatility is dormant (ATR contracting) and event risk is cleared, so the binding constraint is the three-day-weekend gap into Monday. Week ahead: Monday Jun 22 quiet (no first-tier US release); Philly and Richmond Fed surveys Tuesday; the May PCE inflation print Thursday Jun 25 is the week's catalyst.

psychology: The hardest trade on a holiday is no trade. Thin tape invents fake signals: a marginal gold flip, a 68-point NQ chop that feels like structure, an overnight wick that taps a stop and snaps back. None of it is real information. The discipline today is to mark your levels, respect that the trend held without you, and protect capital across a three-day gap. A do-nothing session you sat out flat is a win. Let Monday's full participation give you a real read before you act.

DirectionUPTREND HELD (equities). MNQ and MES stayed LONG above rising ATR stops (MNQ 30,651 vs 30,461; MES 7,557 vs 7,526). No new flip. Gold flipped LONG on its 1hr stop intraday but only marginally and is net -1.30%, a fragile thin-tape flip. Crude firmed +1.30% above 74.74.High / Med
VolatilityCONTRACTING / DORMANT. ATR(14) eased again (MNQ 118.77 to 87.7; MES 21.23 to 15.3). Tiny RTH ranges (MNQ 68 pts, MES 14 pts). Cash VIX did not print (cash closed).High
SessionHOLIDAY HALF-DAY drift. 3 RTH bars, early close 1:00 PM ET. NQ chopped 30,636-30,705 and closed mid-range. No trend day, no clean structure.High
InventoryBALANCED / NEUTRAL. Held the Jun 18 long lean but added nothing; closed near AVWAP. Overnight ran a two-way shakeout (30,388 to 30,771) that resolved flat.Medium
EventPOST_EVENT / holiday lull. No releases today. Monday Jun 22 has no first-tier US release. Next catalyst Thursday Jun 25 (May PCE).High
LiquidityTHIN (holiday). Early close, cash closed, three-day-weekend gap risk into Monday. Lightest tape of the cycle.High

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 18 map, checked on the Jun 19 holiday session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultGrade
1MNQ holds above 30,430 rising ATR stop on hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Held every hourly close (lowest ~30,433 on the overnight dip), closed 30,651. Trend held.
2MNQ loses 30,430 on an hourly closeNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. The overnight wick hit 30,388 but no hourly close broke 30,430. Closing discipline held.
3MES confirms by holding 7,522 stop / 7,500 psychTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Lowest hourly close 7,526 held the stop; 7,500 never threatened. ES stayed long.
4MGC reclaims 4,282 falling stop on an hourly closeNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Gold never neared 4,282. Its 1hr stop flipped long via a lower reclaim (4,196), not this level.
5MCL holds 73.70 rising stop / 75 psychTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Held both easily and firmed to 76.52 (+1.3%). The fragile Thursday long steadied.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Monday, June 22, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Friday June 19 was Juneteenth (cash closed; CME equity futures early close 1:00 PM ET). The map is for the next full session, Monday June 22. Filters Monday: Monday so BIR skipped; HOF, MID, BNC eligible (HOF 12:00-14:00 ET dead zone). Calendar: no first-tier US release Monday; week's focus is May PCE Thursday June 25, Philly and Richmond Fed surveys Tuesday. Treat the post-holiday open with normal three-day-gap caution.

IFTHENBIAS
MNQ holds above 30,461 rising ATR stop on hourly closesUptrend intact, TSMOM-20 long armedConstructive above the stop; first dip toward 30,500 psych is the pullback zone, not a short. Reclaiming 30,705 RTH high / 30,771 overnight high opens 31,000
MNQ loses 30,461 on an hourly closeHoliday repair stalls, back toward chopStand down longs on the stop loss; next refs 30,388 overnight low then prior structure
MES holds 7,526 stop / 7,500 psychBroad risk-on intact, NQ and ES aligned longDips toward the 7,520 overnight low are the pullback zone; losing 7,500 warns of failure
MGC holds above its 4,146 flipped stop on hourly closesFragile gold long persistsOnly a hold keeps the thin-tape long honest; losing 4,146 re-arms the short toward 4,100 psych
MCL holds 74.74 rising stop / 75 psychCrude long intact after the steadyingDips toward 75 are the pullback zone; losing 74.74 flips it back to chop, a no-touch
